title: Riedelia insignis
intro: Riedelia insignis, also known as the "Red Riedelia", is a species of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae, native to Papua New Guinea. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving immune function. Bioactive constituents of Riedelia insignis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to exhibit significant antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The most common herbal preparations made with Riedelia insignis include extracts and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, including fever, respiratory infections, and skin conditions.
